pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh and verifier.py. The original data from ExampleProject confirms that SRS-72, 132, and 137 originally pointed to NEEDS-27, 24, and 21, and were successfully corrupted. However, SRS-106 originally pointed to NEEDS-12 and NEEDS-68, not NEEDS-17. The setup script appends NEEDS-998 to SRS-106 instead of replacing a link. The target of NEEDS-17 for SRS-106 is an artifact of the task instructions, which the verifier correctly validates.
Code analysis of setup_task.sh, confirming changes are applied to documents/SRS.json.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the synthetic ReqView ExampleProject. During setup, four satisfaction links in the SRS document are corrupted. For SRS-72, SRS-132, and SRS-137, their correct links (NEEDS-27, NEEDS-24, and NEEDS-21 respectively) are successfully replaced with invalid targets (NEEDS-999, NEEDS-997, NEEDS-996). For SRS-106, the original targets were NEEDS-12 and NEEDS-68 (not NEEDS-17), so the setup script simply appends the invalid NEEDS-998 target. However, the task specifically instructs the agent to link SRS-106 to NEEDS-17, and the verifier strictly checks that NEEDS-17 is present and the invalid targets are removed across all four requirements.
